text:
Once upon a time, in a sleepy little town dubbed Willow Creek, there lived a wise old owl named Oblio. He had feathers of midnight blue and eyes as deep as noon sun. Every evening, after scanning the forest for threats, he liked to listen quietly.

One particular night, Owlswick. Ow.

A peculiar figure sat by the owl's favorite branches. It fluttered sinister wings.

"Why such pale feathers?" Owlio asked quietly.

"I've seen stars flicker dull."

"You must have stories."

"Yes."

The being leaned in. "There's a lake. Clear."

"All water?"

"Some green."

"The only thing darker."

"A shadow."

"That might think."

"I think I saw."

"A ship."

"Where?"

"In dreams."

You're dreaming."

"I feel... small."

"You're not."

"Why?"

"I dream."

"A moon."

"You?"

"I find the stars."

You're lost.

"Not really."

"I've learned."

I flew.

She smiled.

I'd always knew.
